articles.description: Bypolls in  Assembly seats across  states on April  and  counting on May

articles.title: Voting for assembly bypolls across 6 states on April 9 and April 23, results on May 4: ECI
articles.description: Voting in Goa, Karnataka, Nagaland and Tripura will take place on April 9, while Gujarat and Maharashtra will vote on April 23.

articles.title: Puducherry Goes To Polls On April 9, Votes To Be Counted On May 4
articles.description: Union Territory of Puducherry will go to polls on April 9 for all 30 constituencies. The votes, along with four other states - Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Bengal and Assam - will be counted on May 4.

articles.title: Kerala Goes To Polls On April 9, Results Out On May 4
articles.description: Kerala, which was recently rechristened as Keralam, will vote in a single phase on April 9. The votes for the 140-member assembly will be counted on May 4.

articles.title: Oscars 2026: Nominees, predictions, start time and how to watch
articles.description: The 98th Academy Awards will take place at the Dolby Theatre, with the ceremony set to start at 23:00 GMT.

articles.title: Quote of the day: Martin Luther King Jr on injustice and its ripple effects—‘Injustice anywhere is a threat....'
articles.description: Martin Luther King Jr.’s quote, “Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere,” shows how ignoring injustice in one place weakens justice everywhere. It emphasizes shared responsibility, urging individuals and societies to stand against discrimination, and inequality, wherever they occur.

articles.title: Iranian govt reveals scale of civilian casualties from US-Israeli strikes
articles.description: Govt spokesperson Fatemeh Mohajerani says US-Israeli strikes have damaged more than 42,000 civilian sites across Iran.

articles.title: ECI announces election schedule for 4 States, Puducherry; two-phase polling in West Bengal
articles.description: Kerala, Assam and Puducherry will go to the polls on April 9, while Tamil Nadu will vote in a single phase on April 23. Voting in West Bengal will be held in multiple phases beginning April 23

articles.title: Ex-Pak Captain Sarfaraz Retires, Report Makes Explosive Revelation On Future
articles.description: A source said that the formal announcement will now allow the Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) to appoint him as head coach of the national Test team on a long-term basis.

topStories.title: Iranian govt reveals scale of civilian casualties from US-Israeli strikes
topStories.description: Govt spokesperson Fatemeh Mohajerani says US-Israeli strikes have damaged more than 42,000 civilian sites across Iran.

topStories.title: Iran claims US and Israel using copycat ‘Lucas’ drones to frame it
topStories.description: Iran claims US and Israel using copycat 'Lucas' drones to frame it for regional attacks.

topStories.title: Mamata govt. to clear DA arrears of staff, pensioners
topStories.description: A section of State government employees had moved the Supreme Court on the issue, demanding clearance of DA dues

topStories.title: Trump's personal number is up for sale, report says - and CEOs, crypto investors are paying to get it
topStories.description: Donald Trump's personal mobile number has become Washington DC's most traded commodity, with CEOs offering cash and crypto investors offering cryptocurrency to obtain it, according to an investigation by The Atlantic.

topStories.title: Rahul Gandhi writes to PM Modi, seeks Bharat Ratna for BSP founder Kanshi Ram: ‘Deeply felt’
topStories.description: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi's letter came as the nation commemorated Kanshi Ram's birth anniversary on Sunday, March 15.

topStories.title: Rahul writes to PM Modi, seeks Bharat Ratna for BSP founder Kanshi Ram
topStories.description: As we commemorate the birth anniversary of Kanshi Ramji today and reflect on his legacy and contributions, I write with a request that he be awarded the Bharat Ratna posthumously, says Congress leader Rahul Gandhi

topStories.title: US, China hold trade talks in Paris to clear path to Trump-Xi summit
topStories.description: The world's two largest economies trying to hash out trade issues before US President Trump is expected to visit China.

topStories.title: Bhupinder Hooda confident of Congress win in Rajya Sabha polls in Haryana
topStories.description: In an interview with The Hindu, Mr. Hooda dismissed worries about cross-voting; he slammed Haryana CM Saini, calling him a “CM on deputation”

topStories.title: Rahul Gandhi demands Bharat Ratna for Kanshi Ram, writes to PM Modi as Dalit outreach peaks ahead of UP polls
topStories.description: Move comes as part of Congress outreach to Dalits and other socially backward sections ahead of Uttar Pradesh elections due within a year

topStories.description: US president says his country may carry out more strikes on oil hub as he urges allies to help open Strait of Hormuz.

topStories.description: The Qatar GP, scheduled for April 10-12, has been rescheduled for November amid the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran.

dailyNews.title: Al-Quds Day rally in Toronto calls for an end to the war in Iran & Lebanon
dailyNews.description: Demonstrators gathered in Toronto for Al-Quds Day, denouncing US-Israeli attacks on Iran and Lebanon.

dailyNews.title: US names six crew killed in refuelling plane crash in Iraq
dailyNews.description: The aircraft was on a combat mission as part of ongoing US operations against Iran when it crashed in western Iraq on Thursday.

dailyNews.title: Quote of the day by American poet Robert Frost: ‘I took the one less traveled by…’
dailyNews.description: Robert Frost's famous lines about choosing the unconventional path resonate in today's world, where social media trends dictate lifestyles. The poem emphasizes that choices shape our identity.

dailyNews.title: Republic of Congo votes in election that could extend Sassou’s 42-year rule
dailyNews.description: Incumbent President Denis Sassou Nguesso, 82, is running for fifth term in polls boycotted by major political parties.

dailyNews.title: Iran's revenge for Khamenei? Tehran vows to ‘pursue and kill’ Netanyahu with full force as war enters third week
dailyNews.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has pledged to target Benjamin Netanyahu amidst the ongoing conflict, which has seen at least 15 casualties from a U.S.-Israeli missile strike in Isfahan.

dailyNews.title: Trump seeks warships from other countries to help secure Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: Trump calls for international warships to secure Strait of Hormuz as Iran allegedly threatens global oil shipping routes from the Middle East region.

dailyNews.title: New guard of GOP women say Democrats can't ‘pigeonhole’ female voters as they target key House races
dailyNews.description: A wave of female Republican candidates is working to turn the tables on Democrats' claim to be the party of women and secure strategic victories to help the GOP retain its majority.

dailyNews.title: Restaurants revive retro pricing for anniversaries — but does the trend make ‘cents’?
dailyNews.description: Restaurants nationwide celebrate anniversaries with throwback pricing promotions, offering everything from $1.65 fries to $3.50 pizzas for limited times.

dailyNews.title: Former college volleyball star Kaylie Ray opens up on viral clash with Arizona Democrat senator
dailyNews.description: Former Utah State volleyball player Kaylie Ray confronted Arizona senator Catherine Miranda after the senator questioned Ray's competitiveness during a hearing.

dailyNews.title: 11-year-old accused of premeditated murder in brother’s death can’t be tried as adult, former DA says
dailyNews.description: Shocking homicide case rocks Colorado community as 11-year-old accused of killing younger brother. Juvenile justice system faces rare legal challenge.

dailyNews.title: American Culture Quiz: Test yourself on tipping turmoil and burger buzz
dailyNews.description: The American Culture Quiz is a weekly test of our national traits, trends, history and people. This time, test your knowledge of tipping turmoil, burger buzz and more.

topStories.title: Son of British couple detained in Iran 'let down' by Starmer's leadership on parent's imprisonment amid war
topStories.description: The son of British prisoners in Iran slammed U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer's "non-existent" advocacy and urged President Donald Trump to remember prisoners during strikes on Iran.

topStories.title: For Trump, a Promised Economic Boom Collides With the Costs of War
topStories.description: President Trump had envisioned a growing economy and improving fortunes for American families in 2026. That appears at risk in his war with Iran.

topStories.title: F.C.C. Chair Threatens to Revoke Broadcasters’ Licenses Over War Coverage
topStories.description: The comment from Brendan Carr came on the heels of a social media message from President Trump criticizing the news media’s coverage of the war with Iran.

topStories.title: Trump Administration Turns to Migrant Workers to Help Farm Labor Shortage
topStories.description: As the president’s immigration policies squeeze an already tight supply of farm labor, the Trump administration is making it cheaper to hire foreign farmworkers.

topStories.title: How Minnesota Beat Trump
topStories.description: Moms donating their breast milk to strangers, dads taking someone else’s kids to school: Minnesotans showed a basic human impulse to look out for their neighbors.

dailyNews.title: Trump says he's not ready to make a deal with Iran as Gulf countries report new attacks
dailyNews.description: The war, which shows no signs of ending soon, has upended global air travel, disrupted oil exports from the region and sent fuel prices rising across the world.

dailyNews.title: U.S. intel shows Iran's late leader was wary of son in power, sources say
dailyNews.description: U.S. intelligence has circulated to President Trump's inner circle that Iran's late supreme leader had misgivings about his son replacing him, viewing Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei as not very bright.

dailyNews.title: FCC Chair Brendan Carr says broadcast licenses are not a "property right"
dailyNews.description: In an exclusive interview with CBS News Saturday, Federal Communications Chair Brendan Carr doubled down on his warning that broadcast licenses could be revoked amid President Trump's criticisms of media coverage of the war in Iran.

dailyNews.title: Trump asks China, France, others to help keep Strait of Hormuz open amid war in Iran
dailyNews.description: President Trump called on countries like the U.K., France, South Korea, Japan and even China to help keep the Strait of Hormuz open as Iran tries to strangle the flow of oil from the region. Charlie D'Agata reports.

dailyNews.title: White House proposes new underground visitor screening facility
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration has proposed the construction of an underground facility to screen visitors to the White House.

dailyNews.title: FCC chair threatens broadcast licenses as Trump criticizes Iran war coverage
dailyNews.description: Federal Communications Commission Chair Brendan Carr did not name specific networks, but his post included a reference to a Saturday morning Truth Social post from the president.

dailyNews.title: FCC chair slams broadcasters after Trump disputes reports on Iran-damaged U.S. tankers
dailyNews.description: FCC Chair Brendan Carr blasted broadcasters shortly after President Donald Trump called reports that Iran struck five U.S. tanker planes "fake news."

dailyNews.title: Trump says he thinks Russian leader Putin is helping Iran in war
dailyNews.description: Russian leaders told President Trump they aren't sharing intelligence with Iran as it fights the U.S. and Israel, special envoy Steve Witkoff said this week.

dailyNews.title: Did tariff dividend checks just become more likely? Economists weigh in
dailyNews.description: Months after President Donald Trump suggested sending Americans a $2,000 tariff dividend check, there's a chance households could see that stimulus in 2026.

dailyNews.title: Trump says Iran is ready to negotiate a ceasefire but he's not ready to make a deal
dailyNews.description: In a wide-ranging interview, President Donald Trump said he’s not ready to make a deal to end the war with Iran, despite Iran’s willingness to do so.

dailyNews.title: Trump says there’s been ‘total obliteration’ of Iran militarily
dailyNews.description: President Trump told NBC News’ Kristen Welker in a nearly half hour phone conversation that Iran has been “militarily defeated” and he believes other countries will help to secure passage through the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: Trump says military targets on Iranian island 'obliterated'
dailyNews.description: Trump says military targets on Iranian island 'obliterated'
